1.Title: Response Time Volatility Under Load
1.Insight: For Homepage, response time showed **significant volatility** with peaks up to **4470ms** while virtual users remained almost constant, suggesting the server struggled to consistently handle sustained load.
1.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 4580ms at 12:15 PM UTC to 2960ms at 12:15 PM UTC, stabilized near 2900ms between 12:15 PM UTC and 12:15 PM UTC, then increased to 3560ms at 12:15 PM UTC, and peaked at **4470ms** at 12:16 PM UTC, before decreasing again. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) for the same request remained steady at 10 from 12:15 PM UTC to 12:16 PM UTC, dropping only after 12:17 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider reviewing server-side resource allocation and backend bottlenecks, as consistently high response times under stable concurrency may indicate a need for better scaling or performance tuning.

2.Title: Sharp RPS Decline With User Reduction
2.Insight: Observed a steep **drop in requests per second** for Homepage when virtual users decreased from 10 to 2 after 12:17 PM UTC, highlighting a direct dependency between concurrency and throughput.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated between 2.0 and 4.0 RPS from 12:15 PM UTC to 12:17 PM UTC and then dropped sharply to **0.3 RPS** at 12:17 PM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) for this sampler fell from 10 at 12:16 PM UTC to 2 at 12:17 PM UTC.
2.Recommendation: Review the load test ramp-down logic and ensure the server can deliver consistent throughput even with fewer concurrent users, or adjust test design to better simulate realistic traffic patterns.

3.Title: Transient Response Time Spikes
3.Insight: Noted **transient spikes** in response time reaching **4470ms**, not strictly correlated with higher RPS or virtual user count, suggesting likely server-side processing delays or external bottlenecks.
3.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) spiked to **4470ms** at 12:16 PM UTC while [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained steady. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) did not show corresponding increase, with RPS staying between 2.0 and 4.0.
3.Recommendation: Consider capturing server logs or enabling tracing during peak periods for further diagnosis, focusing on application code paths or external dependencies such as database or API calls.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Client-side response times peaked above 4,000 ms and throughput dropped sharply at the end, suggesting potential backend constraints.

1.Title: Alternating Response Time Pattern
1.Insight: For Homepage, response time showed **alternating rises and falls** despite steady [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics), indicating inconsistent server responsiveness under load.
1.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) ranged from 4580ms at 12:15 PM UTC to a low of 2790ms at 12:16 PM UTC, then peaked again at **4470ms at 12:16 PM UTC** before dropping to 2460ms at 12:17 PM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained constant at 10 until 12:16 PM UTC, then dropped to 2 at 12:17 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) fluctuated between 2.0 and 4.0 RPS until 12:17 PM UTC, then decreased sharply to 0.3 RPS at 12:17 PM UTC.
1.Recommendation: Consider reviewing server performance during periods of response time fluctuation; inconsistent backend scaling or network delays may be contributing. Investigate application logs for spikes and configure more granular monitoring.

2.Title: Sharp Performance Drop at End
2.Insight: A sudden decrease in [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) at **12:17 PM UTC** for Homepage coincided with a drop in response time, likely reflecting reduced load or a recovery after strain.
2.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 9 to 2 at 12:17 PM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) decreased from 3.0 RPS at 12:17 PM UTC to **0.3 RPS at 12:17 PM UTC**. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 3830ms at 12:17 PM UTC to 2460ms at 12:17 PM UTC.
2.Recommendation: Consider investigating whether this drop was intentional (e.g., ramp-down phase), or due to client/server issues; review scaling patterns and test configuration for abrupt changes.

3.Title: Response Time Peaks Despite Stable Load
3.Insight: There were multiple **response time peaks above 4000ms** for Homepage even while [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) were steady, suggesting that the backend may not efficiently handle sustained load.
3.Details: [Response time](#ClientMetrics) peaked at **4360ms at 12:15 PM UTC**, 4080ms at 12:16 PM UTC, and **4470ms at 12:16 PM UTC** with 10 virtual users and RPS between 2.0–4.0. This occurred even as the [virtual users](#ClientMetrics) and [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) metrics were stable.
3.Recommendation: Consider tuning backend capacity or identifying bottlenecks in server components. Add profiling tools to pinpoint high-latency periods.

4.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) completed with performance degradation. Alternating response time spikes and throughput drops were observed, likely indicating backend constraints.
